title: malus baccata subsp. mandshurica
intro: Malus baccata subsp. mandshurica, also known as the Manchurian crabapple, is a subspecies of the Chinese crabapple plant, native to the mountainous regions of northeastern China, Korea, and the Russian Far East. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Malus baccata subsp. mandshurica include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Malus baccata subsp. mandshurica is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in the form of a decoction or powder in traditional herbal medicine.
